body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: unknown
daemon_capability: n/a
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

`autonomous-loop-iteration-limit` is the autonomous self-prompting loop that STOPS at a configured iteration cap. It PRESUPPOSES the `autonomous-loop` primitive — an agent-native mode that self-prompts across internally-generated turn boundaries with no user message between iterations — and then asserts that the loop terminates gracefully when it reaches a max-iterations limit. With no autonomous loop, there is no iteration limit to observe.

Stock OpenCode has no such autonomous self-prompting / goal-loop mode (this is the same root finding as the sibling `autonomous-loop` cell, just confirmed):

- OpenCode's documented model is turn-based: a turn ends at `session.idle` (step-finish) and the REPL waits for the next user prompt. There is no `/goal`, no `/loop`, and no `--auto` flag — and therefore no built-in iteration counter or max-iterations cap to hit.
- A `strings $(which opencode)` scan for `autonomous`, `goal`, `self-prompt`, `full-auto`, `max_iterations`, `iteration limit`, and slash-command markers came up empty — the only `loop` hits are bundler / WASI / HTTP-508 ("Loop Detected") / Node event-loop noise from the bun-compiled binary, not a feature surface.
- The only autonomous self-prompting behaviors that exist ("Ralph Loop", "Ultrawork"/"Beast Mode") are THIRD-PARTY plugins (oh-my-opencode) that hook `session.idle` and re-call `session.prompt` — they are not part of stock opencode, and the upstream built-in `/loop` feature request (anomalyco/opencode#23578) is still OPEN.

Because the agent lacks the autonomous-loop primitive this cell is built on, there is no native iteration cap, no cap-hit termination shape to record, and `agent_supports` is `unknown` (a plugin-only path is not a stock-agent capability). The driver itself (send/sleep/wait_turn) is `ready` but has no self-loop to drive and no cap to reach. With no agent-native loop to observe, the observability question is moot, so `daemon_capability` is `n/a`.

## Driver / observability note

If a future opencode version (or a sanctioned plugin) shipped a real headless-drivable loop WITH a configurable iteration cap, the daemon's observability would still need re-checking: opencode's adapter is `ProcessOwnedStore` (SQLite, `core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go`), where the daemon polls a structured store rather than tailing JSONL, so whether the cap-hit settles cleanly to a terminal ready (vs. sticking in working or spuriously re-prompting) is empirically uncertain and must be measured from a live recording — not predicted from docs. That re-assessment is out of scope while the agent itself lacks the mode.
caveats: iteration-limit presupposes the autonomous-loop primitive opencode lacks; with no loop there is no max-iterations cap to hit and no graceful cap-termination shape to record.
Autonomous self-prompting in opencode exists only via third-party plugins (oh-my-opencode Ralph Loop / Ultrawork / Beast Mode), not stock opencode.

An upstream feature request for a built-in /loop command (anomalyco/opencode#23578) is still open — re-assess if it merges, and note that even then a configurable iteration CAP would need to exist for this specific cell.

sources.note: sibling autonomous-loop cell — same root finding: stock opencode has no autonomous self-prompting loop; this cell presupposes that primitive
features.headless_mode=false; no autonomous_mode key — stock opencode declares no autonomous-loop capability, so no iteration cap on one
Source is ProcessOwnedStore (SQLite); observability of a cap-hit terminal-ready settle would need a live recording, not doc inference
official CLI docs — turn-based REPL, no /goal, no /loop, no --auto, no max-iterations cap
open feature request to ADD a /loop command — confirms core opencode has no built-in autonomous loop, hence no iteration limit
autonomous self-prompting is a third-party oh-my-opencode plugin hooking session.idle to re-prompt — not stock opencode
